statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 20 of 27 countries holding critical-level reserves in at least one fuel type, while crude oil prices remain elevated at $108.17/barrel following Middle East disruptions.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as strategic reserve releases following Middle East supply disruptions have left many countries with dangerously low stock levels. Despite EU averages appearing adequate at 103.8 days for petrol, 96.2 days for diesel, and 90.1 days for jet fuel, the country-level picture reveals widespread vulnerability. A total of 20 of 27 EU countries are operating with critical-level reserves in at least one fuel category, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg showing critical status across all three fuel types.

Jet fuel presents the most acute concern, with 18 countries below the 90-day mandatory minimum and an EU average of just 90.1 days. Romania's jet fuel reserves at 76.4 days and Poland's at just 21.3 days highlight severe aviation fuel shortages. Diesel supplies are also under pressure, with 14 countries in critical status including major economies like Germany (64.5 days), France (83.4 days in warning status), and Italy (64.3 days).

Brent crude oil at $108.17/barrel reflects the 49.2% surge since late February's Middle East escalation, directly impacting pump prices across Europe. The Netherlands leads with petrol at €2.324/liter, while significant price disparities exist with Malta at €1.34/liter. Current elevated oil prices compound the challenge of rebuilding depleted strategic reserves, creating a difficult balancing act between immediate supply needs and long-term security requirements.
keyPoints: 20 of 27 EU countries have critical-level reserves in at least one fuel type, with Romania, Poland, and Luxembourg critical across all three fuels
Jet fuel shows the greatest vulnerability with 18 countries below the 90-day minimum and EU average at just 90.1 days
Diesel reserves are strained in 14 countries including major economies Germany (64.5 days), Italy (64.3 days), and Spain (64.7 days)
Brent crude has surged 49.2% since late February to $108.17/barrel, creating significant cost pressures for reserve rebuilding
Fuel price disparities are extreme, with Netherlands petrol at €2.324/liter versus Malta at €1.34/liter
